data.title: Why Is Aware Stock Falling After Hours Today?
data.description: Shares of Aware (NASDAQ: AWRE) plummeted by over 16% to $1.22 in after-hours trading on Wednesday. The company, specializing in biometric identity and authentication solutions, released its quarterly earnings report, emphasizing its shift towards a platform-driven model. In the first quarter, Aware generated $3.4 million in revenue, down from $3.6 million in the same period last year. Operating expenses surged to $7.0 million from $5.5 million, with around $0.7 million attributed to one-time severance costs related to staff reductions. The net loss expanded to $3.5 million, or $0.16 per share, compared to $1.6 million, or $0.08 per share, in the previous year’s first quarter. Adjusted EBITDA loss worsened to $3.2 million, from $1.5 million in Q1 of 2025.

data.title: Chairman of the Board of Invalda INVL was elected
data.description: During the general shareholders meeting of Invalda INVL on April 30, 2026, Alvydas Banys, Indrė Mišeikytė, and Tomas Bubinas were elected to the Board for a four-year term. Alvydas Banys was subsequently chosen as the Chairman of the Board during the same meeting.

data.title: Eli Lilly Gears Up For Q1 Print; Here Are The Recent Forecast Changes From Wall Street's Most Accurate Analysts
data.description: Eli Lilly and Company is set to reveal its first-quarter earnings on April 30, before the market opens. Analysts are anticipating earnings of $6.79 per share, a significant increase from the previous year's $3.34 per share. The consensus among analysts is that the company will report quarterly revenue of $17.8 billion, compared to $12.73 billion the previous year. In recent developments, Profluent and Eli Lilly have formed a strategic collaboration to develop custom site-specific recombinases for treating genetic diseases. On the trading front, Eli Lilly's stock dipped by 2.6% to close at $851.21 on Wednesday. For more updates and analyst ratings, readers can refer to the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga Pro.

data.title: Facebook, Instagram To Give Creator Payouts In Stablecoin? Here's What Meta's New Announcement Means
data.description: Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ: META) has initiated a new feature offering payouts in USDC (CRYPTO: USDC) to a limited number of content creators, as revealed on the company's website. The tech giant, renowned for owning Facebook and Instagram, announced that this functionality is currently accessible to specific creators based in Colombia and the Philippines. Qualified users have the option to link a cryptocurrency wallet and accept payments in the USDC token, which is pegged to the US dollar, on Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) and Polygon (CRYPTO: POL). Additionally, Meta has provided a comprehensive guide detailing the process for receiving these payouts, encompassing information on wallets, addresses, and the potential risks associated with cryptocurrency transactions. This information was sourced from a report on a well-known financial news platform.

data.title: Ex-day of dividends allocated by the shareholders' meeting of Invalda INVL held on 30/04/2026
data.description: On April 30, 2026, Invalda INVL AB's General Meeting of Shareholders approved the distribution of dividends at EUR 1.00 per share for the year 2025. The total sum designated for dividends amounts to EUR 12.058 million.

data.title: Resolutions of the General Ordinary Shareholders Meeting of INVL Technology
data.description: On April 30, 2026, the General Ordinary Shareholders Meeting of the private equity investment company INVL Technology took place. During the meeting, resolutions were passed regarding the future actions of the company. Key decisions were made concerning the company's operations, investments, and financial strategies. The meeting focused on key issues affecting the company's growth and financial performance. These resolutions will play a crucial role in shaping the company's future direction and market positioning.

data.title: Beijing Launches 'GO BEIJING' for One-Stop Services for Inbound Visitors
data.description: On April 30, 2026, in Beijing, an integrated service platform called "GO BEIJING" was introduced to enhance the experience of foreign visitors. This platform aims to cater to the specific requirements of international guests. The initiative is part of efforts to create a seamless experience for visitors. It encompasses various services and information to facilitate their stay in the city, making it easier for them to navigate and access resources. "GO BEIJING" is a one-stop solution designed to streamline the process for foreign visitors by providing centralized access to essential services. This launch is a significant step towards improving the overall tourism experience, highlighting Beijing's commitment to enhancing hospitality.

data.title: Formosa Pharmaceuticals and Singapore Eye Research Institute (SERI) Partner to Advance Novel Ophthalmic Formulations for Corneal Diseases
data.description: Formosa Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (6838.TW) and the Singapore Eye Research Institute (SERI) have revealed a significant collaboration on April 30, 2026. This partnership aims to work together on advancing ophthalmic formulations. The collaboration is set to bring together the expertise of Formosa and SERI to enhance research capabilities and develop cutting-edge solutions for eye health.
